sources.name: OpenAQ API v3 docs
notes: Official docs cover OpenAQ API version 3.
OpenAQ v1 and v2 were retired on January 31, 2025.
OpenAQ provides public physical pollutant measurements and metadata, not AQI categories.

sources.name: OpenAQ OpenAPI document
notes: OpenAPI 3.1 spec exposes locations, sensors, latest measurements, measurements, parameters, providers, owners, countries, and metadata endpoints.
Location search supports coordinates and radius, with radius capped at 25 km.
Latest measurement endpoints exist by location ID and parameter ID.

sources.name: AirNow API web services docs
notes: Docs list current forecasts, current observations, observations by monitoring site, historical observations, and contour maps.
Current observations can be queried by reporting area, zip code, or latitude/longitude.
Several legacy zip/lat-long services are marked for retirement in fall 2026, so the first print should keep AirNow support narrow and caveated.

sources.name: AirNow API fact sheet
notes: Fact sheet says an AirNow API account is required.
Web service data can be returned in CSV, JSON, or XML.
AirNow recommends file products over web services for large time ranges or broad geographic extracts.

sources.name: AirNow Data Exchange Guidelines
notes: Guidelines apply to AirNow API data.
Observational data are preliminary and subject to change.
AirNow data should not be used for regulatory, trend, guidance, government, or public decision-making.
